elimination_filters.description: Must be EXPLICITLY 100% remote. If remote is not clearly confirmed, REJECT. Junior positions are almost always on-site or hybrid unless they explicitly say remote — do NOT give benefit of the doubt.
elimination_filters.notes: IMPORTANT: If the job does NOT explicitly mention remote/full remote, REJECT it. A city name in the location (e.g. 'Barcelona', 'Berlin', 'Milano') without 'remote' is a strong signal of on-site work — REJECT. Only PASS if remote is explicitly confirmed.

elimination_filters.description: Must accept freelance/B2B/contractor — reject if only permanent employment. Use BOTH direct indicators AND indirect clues.

reject_indirect_indicators.description: These are benefits/perks typically offered ONLY to employees, not freelancers. If 2 or more of these appear together, treat as employment contract and REJECT.

reject_salary_indicators.description: Salary expressed as annual gross (RAL/annual salary/Jahresgehalt) strongly suggests employment, not freelance. Match these only as whole phrases or in clear salary context, NOT as isolated short strings.

elimination_filters.description: ONLY Junior positions. Reject anything that is clearly Mid, Senior, Lead, Staff, Principal, or requires 3+ years of experience. If no seniority is mentioned AND the job requires specific years of experience (e.g. '3+ years'), REJECT.
elimination_filters.notes: If seniority is not mentioned AND no years of experience are required, DO NOT reject. But if the job says '3+ years' or similar WITHOUT saying 'junior', REJECT it.

elimination_filters.description: Reject jobs where German, French, or any non-spoken language is REQUIRED for the role (not just the posting language). The candidate speaks IT/ES/EN only.
elimination_filters.notes: If the posting is in German but says English is the working language, PASS. If German is listed as 'nice to have' or 'von Vorteil', PASS. Only REJECT if German/French is explicitly REQUIRED or MANDATORY for the role.

scoring_criteria.description: How well the required technologies match the candidate's skills. Now includes ALL dev technologies, not just mobile.
scoring_criteria.notes: Core mobile = Swift/Kotlin/ObjC/Java/Flutter/RN. Known tech = PHP/MySQL/Firebase/REST APIs/AWS/GCP/Azure (from profile). Any dev = any software development role (Python, JS, React, Node, etc). Since Junior + AI, candidate can handle any tech.

scoring_criteria.description: Junior only — this is now an elimination filter too, but scoring still differentiates
Language of the job posting and required communication language. German/French postings need explicit confirmation that English is sufficient.
scoring_criteria.notes: If posting is in DE/FR: check if English is confirmed as working language (80pts). If unclear (40pts). If DE/FR is clearly required (10pts, should have been filtered by language_requirement elimination filter).

scoring_criteria.description: How recently the job was posted — THIS IS THE MOST IMPORTANT CRITERION because the goal is to be first to respond
scoring_criteria.notes: If no date is available, assume 'date_unknown' (30 points). Never give high freshness score without a confirmed recent date.

bonus_malus.description: +10 if mentions specific tools candidate knows well: SPM, Jitpack, Fastlane, CI/CD, MVVM, Clean Architecture
+5 if in streaming/media or healthcare/pharma (past experience)
-10 if requires years of experience in tech candidate doesn't know (e.g., Xamarin, .NET MAUI, Ionic)

+20 if the job offers part-time, semi-part-time, or reduced hours. This is HIGHLY preferred. Look for indicators in any language: part-time, part time, parttime, semi-parttime, half-time, 20h/week, 25h/week, 30h/week, media jornada, mezza giornata, Teilzeit, temps partiel, flexible hours, reduced hours, 4-day week, 4 day week.
-15 for jobs sourced from Glassdoor. Glassdoor floods results and many are duplicates of listings on other platforms. Prefer original sources.
